NKJV.1: Am I not an apostle? Am I not free? Have I not seen Jesus Christ our Lord? Are you not my work in the Lord?
NKJV.2: If I am not an apostle to others, yet doubtless I am to you. For you are the seal of my apostleship in the Lord.
NKJV.3: My defense to those who examine me is this:
NKJV.4: Do we have no right to eat and drink?
NKJV.5: Do we have no right to take along a believing wife, as do also the other apostles, the brothers of the Lord, and Cephas?
NKJV.6: Or is it only Barnabas and I who have no right to refrain from working?
NKJV.7: Who ever goes to war at his own expense? Who plants a vineyard and does not eat of its fruit? Or who tends a flock and does not drink of the milk of the flock?
NKJV.8: Do I say these things as a mere man? Or does not the law say the same also?
NKJV.9: For it is written in the law of Moses, “You shall not muzzle an ox while it treads out the grain.” Is it oxen God is concerned about?
NKJV.10: Or does He say it altogether for our sakes? For our sakes, no doubt, this is written, that he who plows should plow in hope, and he who threshes in hope should be partaker of his hope.
NKJV.11: If we have sown spiritual things for you, is it a great thing if we reap your material things?
NKJV.12: If others are partakers of this right over you, are we not even more? Nevertheless we have not used this right, but endure all things lest we hinder the gospel of Christ.
NKJV.13: Do you not know that those who minister the holy things eat of the things of the temple, and those who serve at the altar partake of the offerings of the altar?
NKJV.14: Even so the Lord has commanded that those who preach the gospel should live from the gospel.
NKJV.15: But I have used none of these things, nor have I written these things that it should be done so to me; for it would be better for me to die than that anyone should make my boasting void.
NKJV.16: For if I preach the gospel, I have nothing to boast of, for necessity is laid upon me; yes, woe is me if I do not preach the gospel!
NKJV.17: For if I do this willingly, I have a reward; but if against my will, I have been entrusted with a stewardship.
NKJV.18: What is my reward then? That when I preach the gospel, I may present the gospel of Christ without charge, that I may not abuse my authority in the gospel.
NKJV.19: For though I am free from all men, I have made myself a servant to all, that I might win the more;
NKJV.20: and to the Jews I became as a Jew, that I might win Jews; to those who are under the law, as under the law, that I might win those who are under the law;
NKJV.21: to those who are without law, as without law (not being without law toward God, but under law toward Christ), that I might win those who are without law;
NKJV.22: to the weak I became as weak, that I might win the weak. I have become all things to all men, that I might by all means save some.
NKJV.23: Now this I do for the gospel’s sake, that I may be partaker of it with you.
NKJV.24: Do you not know that those who run in a race all run, but one receives the prize? Run in such a way that you may obtain it.
NKJV.25: And everyone who competes for the prize is temperate in all things. Now they do it to obtain a perishable crown, but we for an imperishable crown.
NKJV.26: Therefore I run thus: not with uncertainty. Thus I fight: not as one who beats the air.
NKJV.27: But I discipline my body and bring it into subjection, lest, when I have preached to others, I myself should become disqualified.

ESV.1: Am I not free? Am I not an apostle? Have I not seen Jesus our Lord? Are not you my workmanship in the Lord?
ESV.2: If to others I am not an apostle, at least I am to you, for you are the seal of my apostleship in the Lord.
ESV.3: This is my defense to those who would examine me.
ESV.4: Do we not have the right to eat and drink?
ESV.5: Do we not have the right to take along a believing wife,  as do the other apostles and the brothers of the Lord and Cephas?
ESV.6: Or is it only Barnabas and I who have no right to refrain from working for a living?
ESV.7: Who serves as a soldier at his own expense? Who plants a vineyard without eating any of its fruit? Or who tends a flock without getting some of the milk?
ESV.8: Do I say these things on human authority? Does not the Law say the same?
ESV.9: For it is written in the Law of Moses, “You shall not muzzle an ox when it treads out the grain.” Is it for oxen that God is concerned?
ESV.10: Does he not certainly speak for our sake? It was written for our sake, because the plowman should plow in hope and the thresher thresh in hope of sharing in the crop.
ESV.11: If we have sown spiritual things among you, is it too much if we reap material things from you?
ESV.12: If others share this rightful claim on you, do not we even more? Nevertheless, we have not made use of this right, but we endure anything rather than put an obstacle in the way of the gospel of Christ.
ESV.13: Do you not know that those who are employed in the temple service get their food from the temple, and those who serve at the altar share in the sacrificial offerings?
ESV.14: In the same way, the Lord commanded that those who proclaim the gospel should get their living by the gospel.
ESV.15: But I have made no use of any of these rights, nor am I writing these things to secure any such provision. For I would rather die than have anyone deprive me of my ground for boasting.
ESV.16: For if I preach the gospel, that gives me no ground for boasting. For necessity is laid upon me. Woe to me if I do not preach the gospel!
ESV.17: For if I do this of my own will, I have a reward, but if not of my own will, I am still entrusted with a stewardship.
ESV.18: What then is my reward? That in my preaching I may present the gospel free of charge, so as not to make full use of my right in the gospel.
ESV.19: For though I am free from all, I have made myself a servant to all, that I might win more of them.
ESV.20: To the Jews I became as a Jew, in order to win Jews. To those under the law I became as one under the law (though not being myself under the law) that I might win those under the law.
ESV.21: To those outside the law I became as one outside the law (not being outside the law of God but under the law of Christ) that I might win those outside the law.
ESV.22: To the weak I became weak, that I might win the weak. I have become all things to all people, that by all means I might save some.
ESV.23: I do it all for the sake of the gospel, that I may share with them in its blessings.
ESV.24: Do you not know that in a race all the runners run, but only one receives the prize? So run that you may obtain it.
ESV.25: Every athlete exercises self-control in all things. They do it to receive a perishable wreath, but we an imperishable.
ESV.26: So I do not run aimlessly; I do not box as one beating the air.
ESV.27: But I discipline my body and keep it under control,  lest after preaching to others I myself should be disqualified.

NASB.1: Am I not free? Am I not an apostle? Have I not seen Jesus our Lord? Are you not my work in the Lord?
NASB.2: If to others I am not an apostle, at least I am to you; for you are the seal of my apostleship in the Lord.
NASB.3: My defense to those who examine me is this:
NASB.4: Do we not have a right to eat and drink?
NASB.5: Do we not have a right to take along a believing wife, even as the rest of the apostles and the brothers of the Lord and Cephas?
NASB.6: Or do only Barnabas and I not have a right to refrain from working?
NASB.7: Who at any time serves as a soldier at his own expense? Who plants a vineyard and does not eat the fruit of it? Or who tends a flock and does not use the milk of the flock?
NASB.8: I am not speaking these things according to human judgment, am I? Or does not the Law also say these things?
NASB.9: For it is written in the Law of Moses, 'YOU SHALL NOT MUZZLE THE OX WHILE HE IS THRESHING.' God is not concerned about oxen, is He?
NASB.10: Or is He speaking altogether for our sake? Yes, for our sake it was written, because the plowman ought to plow in hope, and the thresher [to thresh] in hope of sharing [the crops].
NASB.11: If we sowed spiritual things in you, is it too much if we reap material things from you?
NASB.12: If others share the right over you, do we not more? Nevertheless, we did not use this right, but we endure all things so that we will cause no hindrance to the gospel of Christ.
NASB.13: Do you not know that those who perform sacred services eat the [food] of the temple, [and] those who attend regularly to the altar have their share from the altar?
NASB.14: So also the Lord directed those who proclaim the gospel to get their living from the gospel.
NASB.15: But I have used none of these things. And I am not writing these things so that it will be done so in my case; for it would be better for me to die than have any man make my boast an empty one.
NASB.16: For if I preach the gospel, I have nothing to boast of, for I am under compulsion; for woe is me if I do not preach the gospel.
NASB.17: For if I do this voluntarily, I have a reward; but if against my will, I have a stewardship entrusted to me.
NASB.18: What then is my reward? That, when I preach the gospel, I may offer the gospel without charge, so as not to make full use of my right in the gospel.
NASB.19: For though I am free from all [men], I have made myself a slave to all, so that I may win more.
NASB.20: To the Jews I became as a Jew, so that I might win Jews; to those who are under the Law, as under the Law though not being myself under the Law, so that I might win those who are under the Law;
NASB.21: to those who are without law, as without law, though not being without the law of God but under the law of Christ, so that I might win those who are without law.
NASB.22: To the weak I became weak, that I might win the weak; I have become all things to all men, so that I may by all means save some.
NASB.23: I do all things for the sake of the gospel, so that I may become a fellow partaker of it.
NASB.24: Do you not know that those who run in a race all run, but [only] one receives the prize? Run in such a way that you may win.
NASB.25: Everyone who competes in the games exercises self-control in all things. They then [do it] to receive a perishable wreath, but we an imperishable.
NASB.26: Therefore I run in such a way, as not without aim; I box in such a way, as not beating the air;
NASB.27: but I discipline my body and make it my slave, so that, after I have preached to others, I myself will not be disqualified.

NLT.1: Am I not as free as anyone else? Am I not an apostle? Haven’t I seen Jesus our Lord with my own eyes? Isn’t it because of my work that you belong to the Lord?
NLT.2: Even if others think I am not an apostle, I certainly am to you. You yourselves are proof that I am the Lord’s apostle.
NLT.3: This is my answer to those who question my authority.
NLT.4: Don’t we have the right to live in your homes and share your meals?
NLT.5: Don’t we have the right to bring a believing wife with us as the other apostles and the Lord’s brothers do, and as Peter does?
NLT.6: Or is it only Barnabas and I who have to work to support ourselves?
NLT.7: What soldier has to pay his own expenses? What farmer plants a vineyard and doesn’t have the right to eat some of its fruit? What shepherd cares for a flock of sheep and isn’t allowed to drink some of the milk?
NLT.8: Am I expressing merely a human opinion, or does the law say the same thing?
NLT.9: For the law of Moses says, “You must not muzzle an ox to keep it from eating as it treads out the grain.” Was God thinking only about oxen when he said this?
NLT.10: Wasn’t he actually speaking to us? Yes, it was written for us, so that the one who plows and the one who threshes the grain might both expect a share of the harvest.
NLT.11: Since we have planted spiritual seed among you, aren’t we entitled to a harvest of physical food and drink?
NLT.12: If you support others who preach to you, shouldn’t we have an even greater right to be supported? But we have never used this right. We would rather put up with anything than be an obstacle to the Good News about Christ.
NLT.13: Don’t you realize that those who work in the temple get their meals from the offerings brought to the temple? And those who serve at the altar get a share of the sacrificial offerings.
NLT.14: In the same way, the Lord ordered that those who preach the Good News should be supported by those who benefit from it.
NLT.15: Yet I have never used any of these rights. And I am not writing this to suggest that I want to start now. In fact, I would rather die than lose my right to boast about preaching without charge.
NLT.16: Yet preaching the Good News is not something I can boast about. I am compelled by God to do it. How terrible for me if I didn’t preach the Good News!
NLT.17: If I were doing this on my own initiative, I would deserve payment. But I have no choice, for God has given me this sacred trust.
NLT.18: What then is my pay? It is the opportunity to preach the Good News without charging anyone. That’s why I never demand my rights when I preach the Good News.
NLT.19: Even though I am a free man with no master, I have become a slave to all people to bring many to Christ.
NLT.20: When I was with the Jews, I lived like a Jew to bring the Jews to Christ. When I was with those who follow the Jewish law, I too lived under that law. Even though I am not subject to the law, I did this so I could bring to Christ those who are under the law.
NLT.21: When I am with the Gentiles who do not follow the Jewish law, I too live apart from that law so I can bring them to Christ. But I do not ignore the law of God; I obey the law of Christ.
NLT.22: When I am with those who are weak, I share their weakness, for I want to bring the weak to Christ. Yes, I try to find common ground with everyone, doing everything I can to save some.
NLT.23: I do everything to spread the Good News and share in its blessings.
NLT.24: Don’t you realize that in a race everyone runs, but only one person gets the prize? So run to win!
NLT.25: All athletes are disciplined in their training. They do it to win a prize that will fade away, but we do it for an eternal prize.
NLT.26: So I run with purpose in every step. I am not just shadowboxing.
NLT.27: I discipline my body like an athlete, training it to do what it should. Otherwise, I fear that after preaching to others I myself might be disqualified.

WEB.1: Am I not free? Am I not an apostle? Haven’t I seen Jesus Christ, our Lord? Aren’t you my work in the Lord?
WEB.2: If to others I am not an apostle, yet at least I am to you; for you are the seal of my apostleship in the Lord.
WEB.3: My defense to those who examine me is this.
WEB.4: Have we no right to eat and to drink?
WEB.5: Have we no right to take along a wife who is a believer, even as the rest of the apostles, and the brothers of the Lord, and Cephas?
WEB.6: Or have only Barnabas and I no right to not work?
WEB.7: What soldier ever serves at his own expense? Who plants a vineyard, and doesn’t eat of its fruit? Or who feeds a flock, and doesn’t drink from the flock’s milk?
WEB.8: Do I speak these things according to the ways of men? Or doesn’t the law also say the same thing?
WEB.9: For it is written in the law of Moses, “You shall not muzzle an ox while it treads out the grain.”  Is it for the oxen that God cares,
WEB.10: or does he say it assuredly for our sake? Yes, it was written for our sake, because he who plows ought to plow in hope, and he who threshes in hope should partake of his hope.
WEB.11: If we sowed to you spiritual things, is it a great thing if we reap your fleshly things?
WEB.12: If others partake of this right over you, don’t we yet more? Nevertheless we did not use this right, but we bear all things, that we may cause no hindrance to the Good News of Christ.
WEB.13: Don’t you know that those who serve around sacred things eat from the things of the temple, and those who wait on the altar have their portion with the altar?
WEB.14: Even so the Lord ordained that those who proclaim the Good News should live from the Good News.
WEB.15: But I have used none of these things, and I don’t write these things that it may be done so in my case; for I would rather die, than that anyone should make my boasting void.
WEB.16: For if I preach the Good News, I have nothing to boast about; for necessity is laid on me; but woe is to me, if I don’t preach the Good News.
WEB.17: For if I do this of my own will, I have a reward. But if not of my own will, I have a stewardship entrusted to me.
WEB.18: What then is my reward? That, when I preach the Good News, I may present the Good News of Christ without charge, so as not to abuse my authority in the Good News.
WEB.19: For though I was free from all, I brought myself under bondage to all, that I might gain the more.
WEB.20: To the Jews I became as a Jew, that I might gain Jews; to those who are under the law, as under the law, that I might gain those who are under the law;
WEB.21: to those who are without law, as without law (not being without law toward God, but under law toward Christ), that I might win those who are without law.
WEB.22: To the weak I became as weak, that I might gain the weak. I have become all things to all men, that I may by all means save some.
WEB.23: Now I do this for the sake of the Good News, that I may be a joint partaker of it.
WEB.24: Don’t you know that those who run in a race all run, but one receives the prize? Run like that, that you may win.
WEB.25: Every man who strives in the games exercises self-control in all things. Now they do it to receive a corruptible crown, but we an incorruptible.
WEB.26: I therefore run like that, not aimlessly. I fight like that, not beating the air,
WEB.27: but I beat my body and bring it into submission, lest by any means, after I have preached to others, I myself should be rejected.

CSB17.1: Am I not free? Am I not an apostle? Have I not seen Jesus our Lord? Are you not my work in the Lord?
CSB17.2: If I am not an apostle to others, at least I am to you, because you are the seal <sup>ⓒ</sup> of my apostleship in the Lord.
CSB17.3: My defense to those who examine me is this:
CSB17.4: Don’t we have the right to eat and drink? <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.5: Don’t we have the right to be accompanied by a believing wife <sup>[1]</sup> like the other apostles, <sup>ⓔ</sup> the Lord’s brothers, and Cephas? <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.6: Or do only Barnabas <sup>ⓖ</sup> and I have no right to refrain from working?
CSB17.7: Who serves as a soldier at his own expense? <sup>ⓗ</sup> Who plants a vineyard and does not eat its fruit? Or who shepherds a flock and does not drink the milk from the flock?
CSB17.8: Am I saying this from a human perspective? Doesn’t the law also say the same thing?
CSB17.9: For it is written in the law of Moses, Do not muzzle an ox ⓘ while it treads out grain. [2] ⓙ Is God really concerned about oxen?
CSB17.10: Isn’t he really saying it for our sake? Yes, this is written for our sake, <sup>ⓚ</sup> because he who plows ought to plow in hope, <sup>ⓛ</sup> and he who threshes should thresh in hope of sharing the crop. <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.11: If we have sown spiritual things for you, is it too much if we reap material benefits from you?
CSB17.12: If others have this right to receive benefits from you, don’t we even more? Nevertheless, we have not made use of this right; instead, we endure everything <sup>ⓝ</sup> so that we will not hinder the gospel of Christ.
CSB17.13: Don’t you know that those who perform the temple services eat the food from the temple, and those who serve at the altar <sup>ⓐ</sup> share in the offerings of the altar?
CSB17.14: In the same way, the Lord has commanded that those who preach the gospel should earn their living by the gospel. <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.15: For my part I have used none of these rights, nor have I written these things that they may be applied in my case. For it would be better for me to die than for anyone to deprive me of my boast!
CSB17.16: For if I preach the gospel, I have no reason to boast, because I am compelled to preach <sup>[3]</sup>  — and woe to me if I do not preach the gospel!
CSB17.17: For if I do this willingly, I have a reward, but if unwillingly, I am entrusted <sup>ⓒ</sup> with a commission.
CSB17.18: What then is my reward? To preach the gospel and offer it free of charge and not make full use of my rights in the gospel. <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.19: Although I am free from all and not anyone’s slave, I have made myself a slave to everyone, in order to win <sup>ⓔ</sup> more people.
CSB17.20: To the Jews I became like a Jew, to win Jews; to those under the law, like one under the law — though I myself am not under the law <sup>[4]</sup>  — to win those under the law. <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.21: To those who are without the law, <sup>ⓖ</sup> like one without the law — though I am not without God’s law but under the law of Christ — to win those without the law.
CSB17.22: To the weak I became weak, in order to win the weak. I have become all things to all people, so that I may by every possible means save some. <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.23: Now I do all this because of the gospel, so that I may share in the blessings.
CSB17.24: Don’t you know that the runners in a stadium all race, <sup>ⓘ</sup> but only one receives the prize? Run in such a way to win the prize. <sup>ⓙ</sup>
CSB17.25: Now everyone who competes exercises self-control <sup>ⓚ</sup> in everything. They do it to receive a perishable crown, <sup>ⓛ</sup> but we an imperishable crown.
CSB17.26: So I do not run like one who runs aimlessly or box like one beating the air.
CSB17.27: Instead, I discipline my body and bring it under strict control, so that after preaching to others, I myself will not be disqualified. <sup>ⓜ</sup>

AMP.1: Am I not free [unrestrained and exempt from any obligation]? Am I not an apostle? Have I not seen Jesus our [risen] Lord [in person]? Are you not [the result and proof of] my work in the Lord?
AMP.2: If I am not [considered] an apostle to others, at least I am one to you; for you are the seal and the certificate and the living evidence of my apostleship in the Lord [confirming and authenticating it].
AMP.3: This is my defense to those who would put me on trial and interrogate me [concerning my authority as an apostle]:
AMP.4: Have we not the right to our food and drink [at the expense of the churches]?
AMP.5: Have we not the right to take along with us a believing wife, as do the rest of the apostles and the Lord's brothers and Cephas (Peter)?
AMP.6: Or is it only Barnabas and I who have no right to stop doing manual labor [in order to support our ministry]?
AMP.7: [Consider this:] Who at any time serves as a soldier at his own expense? Who plants a vineyard and does not eat its fruit? Or who tends a flock and does not use the milk of the flock?
AMP.8: Do I say these things only from a man's perspective? Does the Law not endorse the same principles?
AMP.9: For it is written in the Law of Moses, "You shall not muzzle an ox while it is treading out the grain [to keep it from eating the grain]." Is it [only] for oxen that God cares?
AMP.10: Or does He speak entirely for our sake? Yes, it was written for our sake: The plowman ought to plow in hope, and the thresher to thresh in hope of sharing the harvest.
AMP.11: If we have sown [the good seed of] spiritual things in you, is it too much if we reap material things from you?
AMP.12: If others share in this rightful claim over you, do not we even more? However, we did not exercise this right, but we put up with everything so that we will not hinder [the spread of] the good news of Christ.
AMP.13: Do you not know that those who officiate in the sacred services of the temple eat from the temple [offerings of meat and bread] and those who regularly attend the altar have their share from the [offerings brought to the] altar?
AMP.14: So also [on the same principle] the Lord directed those who preach the gospel to get their living from the gospel.
AMP.15: But I have used none of these privileges, nor am I writing this [to suggest] that any such provision be made for me now. For it would be better for me to die than to have anyone deprive me of my boast [in this matter of financial support].
AMP.16: For if I [merely] preach the gospel, I have nothing to boast about, for I am compelled [that is, absolutely obligated to do it]. Woe to me if I do not preach the good news [of salvation]!
AMP.17: For if I do this work of my own free will, then I have a reward; but if it is not of my will [but by God's choosing], I have been entrusted with a [sacred] stewardship.
AMP.18: What then is my reward? [Just this:] that, when I preach the gospel, I may offer the gospel without charge [to everyone], so as not to take advantage of my rights [as a preacher and apostle] in [preaching] the gospel.
AMP.19: For though I am free from all men, I have made myself a slave to everyone, so that I may win more [for Christ].
AMP.20: To the Jews I became as a Jew, so that I might win Jews [for Christ]; to men under the Law, [I became] as one under the Law, though not being under the Law myself, so that I might win those who are under the Law.
AMP.21: To those who are without (outside) the Law, [I became] as one without the Law, though [I am] not without the law of God, but under the law of Christ, so that I might win those who are without law.
AMP.22: To the weak I became [as the] weak, to win the weak. I have become all things to all men, so that I may by all means [in any and every way] save some [by leading them to faith in Jesus Christ].
AMP.23: And I do all this for the sake of the gospel, so that I may share in its blessings along with you.
AMP.24: Do you not know that in a race all the runners run [their very best to win], but only one receives the prize? Run [your race] in such a way that you may seize the prize and make it yours!
AMP.25: Now every athlete who [goes into training and] competes in the games is disciplined and exercises self-control in all things. They do it to win a crown that withers, but we [do it to receive] an imperishable [crown that cannot wither].
AMP.26: Therefore I do not run without a definite goal; I do not flail around like one beating the air [just shadow boxing].
AMP.27: But [like a boxer] I strictly discipline my body and make it my slave, so that, after I have preached [the gospel] to others, I myself will not somehow be disqualified [as unfit for service].

MSG.1: And don't tell me that I have no authority to write like this. I'm perfectly free to do this—isn't that obvious? Haven't I been given a job to do? Wasn't I commissioned to this work in a face-to-face meeting with Jesus, our Master? Aren't you yourselves proof of the good work that I've done for the Master?
MSG.2: Even if no one else admits the authority of my commission, you can't deny it. Why, my work with you is living proof of my authority!
MSG.3: I'm not shy in standing up to my critics.
MSG.4: We who are on missionary assignments for God have a right to decent accommodations,
MSG.5: and we have a right to support for us and our families. You don't seem to have raised questions with the other apostles and our Master's brothers and Peter in these matters.
MSG.6: So, why me? Is it just Barnabas and I who have to go it alone and pay our own way?
MSG.7: Are soldiers self-employed? Are gardeners forbidden to eat vegetables from their own gardens? Don't milkmaids get to drink their fill from the pail?
MSG.8: I'm not just sounding off because I'm irritated. This is all written in the scriptural law.
MSG.9: Moses wrote, "Don't muzzle an ox to keep it from eating the grain when it's threshing." Do you think Moses' primary concern was the care of farm animals?
MSG.10: Don't you think his concern extends to us? Of course. Farmers plow and thresh expecting something when the crop comes in.
MSG.11: So if we have planted spiritual seed among you, is it out of line to expect a meal or two from you?
MSG.12: Others demand plenty from you in these ways. Don't we who have never demanded deserve even more? But we're not going to start demanding now what we've always had a perfect right to. Our decision all along has been to put up with anything rather than to get in the way or detract from the Message of Christ.
MSG.13: All I'm concerned with right now is that you not use our decision to take advantage of others, depriving them of what is rightly theirs. You know, don't you, that it's always been taken for granted that those who work in the Temple live off the proceeds of the Temple, and that those who offer sacrifices at the altar eat their meals from what has been sacrificed?
MSG.14: Along the same lines, the Master directed that those who spread the Message be supported by those who believe the Message.
MSG.15: Still, I want it made clear that I've never gotten anything out of this for myself, and that I'm not writing now to get something. I'd rather die than give anyone ammunition to discredit me or impugn my motives.
MSG.16: If I proclaim the Message, it's not to get something out of it for myself. I'm compelled to do it, and doomed if I don't!
MSG.17: If this was my own idea of just another way to make a living, I'd expect some pay. But since it's not my idea but something solemnly entrusted to me, why would I expect to get paid?
MSG.18: So am I getting anything out of it? Yes, as a matter of fact: the pleasure of proclaiming the Message at no cost to you. You don't even have to pay my expenses!
MSG.19: Even though I am free of the demands and expectations of everyone, I have voluntarily become a servant to any and all in order to reach a wide range of people:
MSG.22: the defeated, the demoralized—whoever. I didn't take on their way of life. I kept my bearings in Christ—but I entered their world and tried to experience things from their point of view. I've become just about every sort of servant there is in my attempts to lead those I meet into a God-saved life.
MSG.23: I did all this because of the Message. I didn't just want to talk about it; I wanted to be in on it!
MSG.24: You've all been to the stadium and seen the athletes race. Everyone runs; one wins. Run to win.
MSG.25: All good athletes train hard. They do it for a gold medal that tarnishes and fades. You're after one that's gold eternally.
MSG.26: I don't know about you, but I'm running hard for the finish line. I'm giving it everything I've got. No sloppy living for me!
MSG.27: I'm staying alert and in top condition. I'm not going to get caught napping, telling everyone else all about it and then missing out myself.

NIV.1:
The Rights of an Apostle
Am I not free? Am I not an apostle? Have I not seen Jesus our Lord? Are you not the result of my work in the Lord?
NIV.2: Even though I may not be an apostle to others, surely I am to you! For you are the seal of my apostleship in the Lord.
NIV.3: This is my defense to those who sit in judgment on me.
NIV.4: Don’t we have the right to food and drink?
NIV.5: Don’t we have the right to take a believing wife along with us, as do the other apostles and the Lord’s brothers and Cephas?
NIV.6: Or is it only I and Barnabas who must work for a living?
NIV.7: Who serves as a soldier at his own expense? Who plants a vineyard and does not eat of its grapes? Who tends a flock and does not drink of the milk?
NIV.8: Do I say this merely from a human point of view? Doesn’t the Law say the same thing?
NIV.9: For it is written in the Law of Moses: “Do not muzzle an ox while it is treading out the grain.” Is it about oxen that God is concerned?
NIV.10: Surely he says this for us, doesn’t he? Yes, this was written for us, because when the plowman plows and the thresher threshes, they ought to do so in the hope of sharing in the harvest.
NIV.11: If we have sown spiritual seed among you, is it too much if we reap a material harvest from you?
NIV.12:
If others have this right of support from you, shouldn’t we have it all the more?
But we did not use this right. On the contrary, we put up with anything rather than hinder the gospel of Christ.
NIV.13: Don’t you know that those who work in the temple get their food from the temple, and those who serve at the altar share in what is offered on the altar?
NIV.14: In the same way, the Lord has commanded that those who preach the gospel should receive their living from the gospel.
NIV.15: But I have not used any of these rights. And I am not writing this in the hope that you will do such things for me. I would rather die than have anyone deprive me of this boast.
NIV.16: Yet when I preach the gospel, I cannot boast, for I am compelled to preach. Woe to me if I do not preach the gospel!
NIV.17: If I preach voluntarily, I have a reward; if not voluntarily, I am simply discharging the trust committed to me.
NIV.18: What then is my reward? Just this: that in preaching the gospel I may offer it free of charge, and so not make use of my rights in preaching it.
NIV.19: Though I am free and belong to no man, I make myself a slave to everyone, to win as many as possible.
NIV.20: To the Jews I became like a Jew, to win the Jews. To those under the law I became like one under the law (though I myself am not under the law), so as to win those under the law.
NIV.21: To those not having the law I became like one not having the law (though I am not free from God’s law but am under Christ’s law), so as to win those not having the law.
NIV.22: To the weak I became weak, to win the weak. I have become all things to all men so that by all possible means I might save some.
NIV.23: I do all this for the sake of the gospel, that I may share in its blessings.
NIV.24: Do you not know that in a race all the runners run, but only one gets the prize? Run in such a way as to get the prize.
NIV.25: Everyone who competes in the games goes into strict training. They do it to get a crown that will not last; but we do it to get a crown that will last forever.
NIV.26: Therefore I do not run like a man running aimlessly; I do not fight like a man beating the air.
NIV.27: No, I beat my body and make it my slave so that after I have preached to others, I myself will not be disqualified for the prize.

NRSVCE.1: Am I not free? Am I not an apostle? Have I not seen Jesus our Lord? Are you not my work in the Lord?
NRSVCE.2: If I am not an apostle to others, at least I am to you; for you are the seal of my apostleship in the Lord.
NRSVCE.3: This is my defense to those who would examine me.
NRSVCE.4: Do we not have the right to our food and drink?
NRSVCE.5: Do we not have the right to be accompanied by a believing wife, as do the other apostles and the brothers of the Lord and Cephas?
NRSVCE.6: Or is it only Barnabas and I who have no right to refrain from working for a living?
NRSVCE.7: Who at any time pays the expenses for doing military service? Who plants a vineyard and does not eat any of its fruit? Or who tends a flock and does not get any of its milk?
NRSVCE.8: Do I say this on human authority? Does not the law also say the same?
NRSVCE.9: For it is written in the law of Moses, “You shall not muzzle an ox while it is treading out the grain.” Is it for oxen that God is concerned?
NRSVCE.10: Or does he not speak entirely for our sake? It was indeed written for our sake, for whoever plows should plow in hope and whoever threshes should thresh in hope of a share in the crop.
NRSVCE.11: If we have sown spiritual good among you, is it too much if we reap your material benefits?
NRSVCE.12: If others share this rightful claim on you, do not we still more? Nevertheless, we have not made use of this right, but we endure anything rather than put an obstacle in the way of the gospel of Christ.
NRSVCE.13: Do you not know that those who are employed in the temple service get their food from the temple, and those who serve at the altar share in what is sacrificed on the altar?
NRSVCE.14: In the same way, the Lord commanded that those who proclaim the gospel should get their living by the gospel.
NRSVCE.15: But I have made no use of any of these rights, nor am I writing this so that they may be applied in my case. Indeed, I would rather die than that — no one will deprive me of my ground for boasting!
NRSVCE.16: If I proclaim the gospel, this gives me no ground for boasting, for an obligation is laid on me, and woe to me if I do not proclaim the gospel!
NRSVCE.17: For if I do this of my own will, I have a reward; but if not of my own will, I am entrusted with a commission.
NRSVCE.18: What then is my reward? Just this: that in my proclamation I may make the gospel free of charge, so as not to make full use of my rights in the gospel.
NRSVCE.19: For though I am free with respect to all, I have made myself a slave to all, so that I might win more of them.
NRSVCE.20: To the Jews I became as a Jew, in order to win Jews. To those under the law I became as one under the law (though I myself am not under the law) so that I might win those under the law.
NRSVCE.21: To those outside the law I became as one outside the law (though I am not free from God’s law but am under Christ’s law) so that I might win those outside the law.
NRSVCE.22: To the weak I became weak, so that I might win the weak. I have become all things to all people, that I might by all means save some.
NRSVCE.23: I do it all for the sake of the gospel, so that I may share in its blessings.
NRSVCE.24: Do you not know that in a race the runners all compete, but only one receives the prize? Run in such a way that you may win it.
NRSVCE.25: Athletes exercise self-control in all things; they do it to receive a perishable wreath, but we an imperishable one.
NRSVCE.26: So I do not run aimlessly, nor do I box as though beating the air;
NRSVCE.27: but I punish my body and enslave it, so that after proclaiming to others I myself should not be disqualified.

NET.1: Am I not free? Am I not an apostle? Have I not seen Jesus our Lord? Are you not my work in the Lord?
NET.2: If I am not an apostle to others, at least I am to you, for you are the confirming sign of my apostleship in the Lord.
NET.3: This is my defense to those who examine me.
NET.4: Do we not have the right to financial support?
NET.5: Do we not have the right to the company of a believing wife, like the other apostles and the Lord’s brothers and Cephas?
NET.6: Or do only Barnabas and I lack the right not to work?
NET.7: Who ever serves in the army at his own expense? Who plants a vineyard and does not eat its fruit? Who tends a flock and does not consume its milk?
NET.8: Am I saying these things only on the basis of common sense, or does the law not say this as well?
NET.9: For it is written in the law of Moses, “Do not muzzle an ox while it is treading out the grain.” God is not concerned here about oxen, is he?
NET.10: Or is he not surely speaking for our benefit? It was written for us, because the one plowing and threshing ought to work in hope of enjoying the harvest.
NET.11: If we sowed spiritual blessings among you, is it too much to reap material things from you?
NET.12: If others receive this right from you, are we not more deserving? But we have not made use of this right. Instead we endure everything so that we may not be a hindrance to the gospel of Christ.
NET.13: Don’t you know that those who serve in the temple eat food from the temple, and those who serve at the altar receive a part of the offerings?
NET.14: In the same way the Lord commanded those who proclaim the gospel to receive their living by the gospel.
NET.15: But I have not used any of these rights. And I am not writing these things so that something will be done for me. In fact, it would be better for me to die than — no one will deprive me of my reason for boasting!
NET.16: For if I preach the gospel, I have no reason for boasting, because I am compelled to do this. Woe to me if I do not preach the gospel!
NET.17: For if I do this voluntarily, I have a reward. But if I do it unwillingly, I am entrusted with a responsibility.
NET.18: What then is my reward? That when I preach the gospel I may offer the gospel free of charge, and so not make full use of my rights in the gospel.
NET.19: For since I am free from all I can make myself a slave to all, in order to gain even more people.
NET.20: To the Jews I became like a Jew to gain the Jews. To those under the law I became like one under the law (though I myself am not under the law) to gain those under the law.
NET.21: To those free from the law I became like one free from the law (though I am not free from God’s law but under the law of Christ) to gain those free from the law.
NET.22: To the weak I became weak in order to gain the weak. I have become all things to all people, so that by all means I may save some.
NET.23: I do all these things because of the gospel, so that I can be a participant in it.
NET.24: Do you not know that all the runners in a stadium compete, but only one receives the prize? So run to win.
NET.25: Each competitor must exercise self-control in everything. They do it to receive a perishable crown, but we an imperishable one.
NET.26: So I do not run uncertainly or box like one who hits only air.
NET.27: Instead I subdue my body and make it my slave, so that after preaching to others I myself will not be disqualified.
